content:
Hello gents...
Time to tighten up the plan for a Beta release date.
Lets go around the horn again and poll status of the code base. Leaving aside (for the moment) the User Manual refactor and update, who is working on a feature/enhancement/bug-fix that really needs to be in O5.10?

For me, I'm done with big visible feature-like things. Plowing through the github issues list, one-by-one.
Others?
I still think I will be able to finish https://github.com/OpenCPN/OpenCPN/issues/3182 over the weekend, that is the last bigger thing I am working on for 5.10
@Pavel Kalian Any news on #3182?

CirclleCI macOS builds fail because of the brownout before Intel builders are decomisioned next week. Tha catch is they did not make the M1 builders available to the free plans. Let's hope they will and the builds should probably just keep working.

Just about ready to tag and publish Beta-2.
Status check all around, please.
Any blocking tasks?
OK. Let us not forget new package build chain check

I think I've found the bug for this CF post
PR to come soon.

Blocking task?
If the first start wizard crash on my RPi's is not a show stopper? Or is just my system?

The stacktrace you posted does not seem to have anything to do with the wizard (looks to be in the check for multiple OpenCPN instances running at the same time) and I can't reproduce it. If you try to pinpoint what exactly makes it crash, we may fix it.

My Linux knowledge is low. I can only state that wizard crashes on both RPi4 and RPi5. If I comment out EnumerateTCP() it doesn't crash.
I tried to use gdb to find the cause but that was obviously not good enough? Any advice?
Add some debug printouts in the code to narrow down where the crash actually happens, EnumerateTCP() is too big of a target to guess why it crashes for you.
Now no crash on my Rpi using wizard, so if this was hindering Beta2 it's fixed.

@Alec Leamas
Beta-2 is ready to build.
Tag is "v5.9.2-beta2", on branch v5.9.x. CCI builds are all clean.
Please advise when Beta-2 for bookworm is ready.
@Dave Register I'm on it, starting with the Ubuntu branches

Beta-2 is ready to build.
Tag is "v5.9.2-beta2", on branch v5.9.x. CCI builds are all clean.

hm... that tag does not exist on upstream. Did you forget to push it?

Taking a break now, will need to rebuild sid and noble (my bad)

Tag is "v5.9.2-beta2", on branch v5.9.x

sidenote: tags are actually unrelated to branches.
@Dave Register ^^

@Dave Register

Still asleep? I'll assume that the missing tag is pointing to the tip of the v5.9..x branch i. .e. b2dfeef58

Awake now.
Pushed the tag. Sorry.
NP, I made the correct bet. stay tuned.
I'm out for a couple of hours. Be back...

First batch released by you are ready.

The branches ubuntu/numbat and ubuntu/jammy are updated and ready for debuild -S.

Noted that you merged the flathub PR.

Remaining part is the builds published by me which eventually will end up in Debian. Taking a break now, might find time later today.

"ready for debuild -S."
Where do we build these?

Alec....
I still do not understand clearly the planned distribution mechanism for linux.
I understand that bookworm and derivatives (mainly Jammy) will be by "official bookworm backports".
What about Focal and numbats? Will we host some repo somewhere, ala crabdance?
Or what?
We use the PPÅ more or less as today. That is, you make 'debuild -S' which creates a source package and then uploads this using 'dput'.
On phone w clumsy fingers :(
Nothing changes for Ubuntu users, they use the PPÅ as today.

OK, this is all new to me. Or if we talked before, I lost it...

Simple thing first.

dsr@debian:~/Projects/opencpn$ git checkout -b ubuntu/jammy
Switched to a new branch 'ubuntu/jammy'
dsr@debian:~/Projects/opencpn$ git pull github ubuntu/jammy
.
.
.
From github.com:OpenCPN/OpenCPN

  • branch                ubuntu/jammy -> FETCH_HEAD
  • [new branch]          ubuntu/jammy -> github/ubuntu/jammy
    fatal: refusing to merge unrelated histories

Hmmm...
How do I checkout the new branch at the proper tag?

Short answer: there is no need for that, The branches are at the proper tag, they are already merged. The only things to do are.

  1. debuild -S.- This creates a source.changes file in the parent directory a k a ..
  2. dput ppa:bdbcat/opencpn ppa:bdbcat/opencpn ../opencpn_5.9.2-beta2+dfsg-1ubuntu1~bpo2204.1_source.changes. The actual filename willl vary depending on branch
I checked out ubuntu/jammy. HEAD of this branch is several commits beyond the Beta2 tag, now.
Something wrong with my git-fu.

HEAD of this branch is several commits beyond the Beta2 tag, now.

Yes. But iff you look into it, you will find that the only differences are about the debian/ directory. The toolchain checks this bu comparing the sources with the tarball contents at each build
So I can debuild from master HEAD?
No, you don't have a usable debian/ directory in master. Furthermore, you cannot have since the debian directory differs for noble, jammy, bookworm, sid, etc.

Note: My git commands above failed "fatal, refusing to merge....
So I got nothing, I think.
Yes. Merges are evil since there is no git merge --abort. I tend to avoid them, rebase is almost always better.
The easiest might be to clone again?
... if there are problems, that is.

A fresh clone worked, but I'd rather not switch just now.
What went wrong with my original git stuff, and how best to fix?

It went wrong when you tried to merge. You might be able to fix it using something like git reset --hard origin/ubuntu/noble. I don't know which branch you are on or the name of your remote so just a sketch

EDIT: Trying to improve command

I am working on ubuntu/jammy, remote name is "github".

$ git reset --hard github/ubuntu/jammy

fixed it.
OK , great. Now, no more commits, please :)

debuild -S fails:

dsr@debian:~/Projects/opencpn/build_jammy$ debuild -S
dpkg-buildpackage -us -uc -ui -S
dpkg-buildpackage: info: source package opencpn
dpkg-buildpackage: info: source version 5.9.2-beta2+dfsg-1ubuntu1~bpo2204.1
dpkg-buildpackage: info: source distribution jammy
dpkg-buildpackage: info: source changed by Alec Leamas <leamas.alec@gmail.com>
dpkg-source --before-build .
debian/rules clean
dh clean
  dh_clean
dpkg-source -b .
dpkg-source: error: can't build with source format '3.0 (quilt)': no upstream tarball found at ../opencpn_5.9.2-beta2+dfsg.orig.tar.{bz2,gz,lzma,xz}
dpkg-buildpackage: error: dpkg-source -b . subprocess returned exit status 25
debuild: fatal error at line 1182:
dpkg-buildpackage -us -uc -ui -S failed
d

Ah... since you did not make a test build. Sorry, my bad. Re-create the missing tarball like this:

$ git fetch github pristine-tar:pristine-tar
$ git switch pristine-tar
$ pristine-tar checkout opencpn_5.9.2-beta2+dfsg.orig.tar.xz
$ mv opencpn_5.9.2-beta2+dfsg.orig.tar.xz ..
$ git switch ubuntu/jammy

This is a one-time setup, when you later build for noble you use the same tarball
BTW: pristine-tar listlists available tarballs, there is no way to deduce those filenames...

dsr@debian:~/Projects/opencpn/build_jammy$ pristine-tar checkout opencpn_5.9.2-beta2+dfsg.orig.tar.xz
pristine-tar: cannot determine type of vcs used for the current directory
hm... are you in the build directory? If so, try to move to ~/Projects/opencpn
... i. e., cd ~/Projects/opencpn

OK, that worked. I seem to have the pristine tar. But, still fails.

dsr@debian:~/Projects/opencpn/build_jammy$ debuild -S
dpkg-buildpackage -us -uc -ui -S
dpkg-buildpackage: info: source package opencpn
dpkg-buildpackage: info: source version 5.9.2-beta2+dfsg-1ubuntu1~bpo2204.1
dpkg-buildpackage: info: source distribution jammy
dpkg-buildpackage: info: source changed by Alec Leamas <leamas.alec@gmail.com>
dpkg-source --before-build .
debian/rules clean
dh clean
  dh_clean
dpkg-source -b .
dpkg-source: error: can't build with source format '3.0 (quilt)': no upstream tarball found at ../opencpn_5.9.2-beta2+dfsg.orig.tar.{bz2,gz,lzma,xz}
dpkg-buildpackage: error: dpkg-source -b . subprocess returned exit status 25
debuild: fatal error at line 1182:
dpkg-buildpackage -us -uc -ui -S failed

dsr@debian:~/Projects/opencpn/build_jammy$ ls ../opencpn_5.9.2-beta2+dfsg.orig.tar.xz
../opencpn_5.9.2-beta2+dfsg.orig.tar.xz
dsr@debian:~/Projects/opencpn/build_jammy$
As it says: it wants the tarball in .. i. e., ~/Projects
That is, move the xz file created by pristine-tar to ~/Projects first

Yes, this will create various generated stuff in ~/Projects. That's the reason I recommend a nested structure like ~/Projects/opencpn/opencpn. The git repo is opencpn/opencpn, plain opencpn is where the tarball and generated stuff goes.

Yes, it starts to look like a clean clone is the best way. debuild complains about all of the other stuff (e.g. build dirs, ide config files, etc) in my Projects/opencpn directory. Literally thousands of error generated. Sigh.

dpkg-source: info: using source format '3.0 (quilt)'
dpkg-source: info: building opencpn using existing ./opencpn_5.9.2-beta2+dfsg.orig.tar.xz
dpkg-source: info: using patch list from debian/patches/series
dpkg-source: error: cannot represent change to ._.DS_Store: binary file contents changed
dpkg-source: error: add ._.DS_Store in debian/source/include-binaries if you want to store the modified binary in the debian tarball
dpkg-source: warning: executable mode 0755 of '._.DS_Store' will not be represented in diff
dpkg-source: error: cannot represent change to ._COPYING.gplv2: binary file contents changed
dpkg-source: error: add ._COPYING.gplv2 in debian/source/include-binaries if you want to store the modified binary in the debian tarball
dpkg-source: warning: executable mode 0755 of '._COPYING.gplv2' will not be represented in diff
dpkg-source: warning: executable mode 0755 of '.directory' will not be represented in diff
dpkg-source: warning: file opencpn/.idea/.name has no final newline (either original or modified version)
dpkg-source: warning: file opencpn/.idea/codeStyles/Project.xml has no final newline (either original or modified version)
dpkg-source: warning: file opencpn/.idea/codeStyles/codeStyleConfig.xml has no final newline (either original or modified version)
dpkg-source: warning: file opencpn/.idea/misc.xml has no final newline (either original or modified version)
dpkg-source: warning: file opencpn/.idea/modules.xml has no final newline (either original or modified version)
dpkg-source: warning: file opencpn/.idea/opencpn.iml has no final newline (either original or modified version)
dpkg-source: warning: file opencpn/.idea/vcs.xml has no final newline (either original or modified version)
dpkg-source: warning: file opencpn/.idea/workspace.xml has no final newline (either original or modified version)
dpkg-source: error: cannot represent change to build/.DS_Store: binary file contents changed
dpkg-source: error: add build/.DS_Store in debian/source/include-binaries if you want to store the modified binary in the debian tarball
dpkg-sou
Yes. When cloning, make sure you actually clone into opencpn/opencpn, see above
